title: Pakistani Chicken Karahi

subs.name: 3 tbsp full-fat coconut milk
subs.notes: changes character slightly but keeps richness

ingredients.name: fresh coriander and ginger strips for garnish

steps.text: Heat oil in a wide karahi or heavy wok over high flame. Add cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 15 seconds, then add the minced garlic and half the ginger julienne. Fry, stirring constantly, for 1 minute until fragrant but not burnt.

Add the chicken pieces and sear over high heat, turning every minute, until all surfaces are sealed and lightly golden — about 7–8 minutes. The chicken will release moisture; keep the flame high so it evaporates quickly.

Add the chopped tomatoes, red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Stir vigorously and cook uncovered on medium-high heat for 10 minutes, pressing the tomatoes with the back of the spoon until they break down completely into a thick paste.

Reduce heat to medium. Stir in the beaten curd one spoon at a time to prevent splitting. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring often, until the yogurt is absorbed and the oil begins to separate around the edges of the masala.

Add the green chillies and coarsely ground black pepper. Continue cooking for another 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the masala dries out and clings thickly to each piece of chicken and oil pools visibly on top.
Sprinkle garam masala over the top and fold through gently. Taste and adjust salt. Finish with remaining ginger julienne and fresh coriander leaves. Serve straight from the karahi with naan or roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → 750 g mutton (curry cut) (increase cook time to 60 minutes); curd → 3 tbsp full-fat coconut milk (changes character slightly but keeps richness); green-chilli → 1 tsp chilli flakes (for milder heat)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This dish translates perfectly to an Indian kitchen. Use a cast-iron kadai or heavy wok for authentic char. Kashmiri red chilli powder gives the characteristic deep colour without extra heat. The dish requires no special Pakistani pantry items — standard Indian grocery spices work exactly the same. For a quick weeknight version, pressure cook the chicken for 2 whistles first, then finish the masala in the kadai.
